headlines.title: Murdered student Henry Nowak told police 'I can't breathe' while handcuffed
headlines.description: An independent investigation into why Henry Nowak was handcuffed by police as he was dying is ongoing.

headlines.title: UK Athletics fined £350,000 over death of Paralympian
headlines.description: A judge at the Old Bailey has fined UK Athletics £350,000 with £44,000 in costs after hearing how a paralympic athlete died when equipment fell on him at a training ground in east London.

headlines.title: How pupils with special educational needs are more likely to see their schools close
headlines.description: In schools that closed between 2020 and 2025, nearly 30% of children had special educational needs

headlines.title: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble after Russian strikes kill 18 people
headlines.description: An eight-year-old boy and a woman killed in a strike on an apartment block are among the dead, officials say.

headlines.title: Third of people say uni degree not worth it, as student loan inquiry begins
headlines.description: MPs will hear the concerns of graduates about the size of their student debts, and the interest rates.

headlines.title: Scotland legend Sir Kenny Dalglish undergoing treatment for cancer
headlines.description: Sir Kenny Dalglish confirms he is undergoing treatment for cancer.

headlines.title: Instagram AI chatbot tricked by hackers to give access to others' accounts
headlines.description: Some reports have linked the incident to recent cases of high-profile Instagram accounts being hijacked.

headlines.title: Sabrina Carpenter gets restraining order against man who showed up at her home
headlines.description: The singer reported a series of "deeply alarming" incidents at her house to Los Angeles police.

headlines.title: How former SNP chief executive Peter Murrell spent the £400,000 he embezzled
headlines.description: BBC Scotland News has collated all of the goods bought by ex-SNP chief executive Peter Murrell in his £400,000 embezzlement.

headlines.title: Cara Delevingne says new music was inspired by getting sober
headlines.description: The model and actress is now moving into the world of music, having released her first two songs.

headlines.title: Post Office scandal victim dedicates OBE to 'sub-postmasters we have lost'
headlines.description: The 92-year-old said she dedicate the OBE to 'all sub-postmasters we have lost'.

headlines.title: Can a reality show villain become mayor of one of America's largest cities?
headlines.description: Republican Spencer Pratt, known for his TV role on The Hills, appears to be in a tight race with two Democrats.

headlines.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
headlines.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

headlines.title: 'Mornings and nights no longer exist' at 47C:  A day in the hottest place in India
headlines.description: What a day in India's hottest district reveals about life on the frontline of extreme heat.

headlines.title: Toy Story 5 shows 'terror' of children's screen addiction, says Tom Hanks
headlines.description: The latest film sees Woody, Buzz and the other toys compete with a new tablet for children's attention.

headlines.title: Paralympian could become first astronaut with disability to live and work in space
headlines.description: John McFall takes one step closer to becoming first disabled astronaut.

headlines.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
headlines.description: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

headlines.title: Genital herpes rising in England, despite overall drop in STIs
headlines.description: STIs are particularly common among young people, with health experts saying testing for them is vital.

headlines.title: Missing lab worker found dead in New Mexico nearly a year after disappearing
headlines.description: Melissa Casias' case was part of debunked speculations about recent deaths and disappearances in the US scientific community.

headlines.title: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
headlines.description: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

headlines.title: Former Lioness Jill Scott to do 388-mile cycle and run challenge for Sport Relief
headlines.description: Along the way, she will stop at football stadiums and hubs that have been a part of her life.

headlines.title: AI giant Anthropic plans to sell shares in US as valuation nears $1tn
headlines.description: The AI company behind Claude is set to offer the public the chance to buy and sell shares in the firm later this year.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Newscast
headlines.description: More than 1000 documents on Mandelson’s appointment as ambassador published.

headlines.title: Does the US need new laws to protect elections?
headlines.description: And, why does Donald Trump want to “nationalise” elections?

headlines.title: Sir Kenny Dalglish undergoing treatment for cancer
headlines.description: Sir Kenny Dalglish confirms he is undergoing treatment for cancer.

headlines.title: Liverpool in advanced talks with Iraola
headlines.description: Liverpool are in advanced talks with Andoni Iraola as they look to appoint a new head coach following the sacking of Arne Slot.

headlines.title: UK Athletics fined £350,000 over death of Paralympian
headlines.description: A judge at the Old Bailey has fined UK Athletics £350,000 with £44,000 in costs after hearing how a paralympic athlete died when equipment fell on him at a training ground in east London.

headlines.title: Fulham boss Silva close to Benfica agreement
headlines.description: Fulham head coach Marco Silva is close to reaching an agreement to take charge at Benfica.

headlines.title: Southampton owner will not sack head coach Eckert
headlines.description: Southampton owner Dragan Solak tells BBC Sport he will not sack Tonda Eckert, despite the "mistake" the head coach made when authorising a spying campaign against rival Championship clubs.

headlines.title: World Cup 2026: How to follow on the BBC
headlines.description: The biggest World Cup in history starts on 11 June and there are more ways to follow it on the BBC than ever before.
